entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop produced by smallholder farmers in Nigeria.

entities.name: GENDER YIELD GAP
entities.description: The difference in crop yield between male and female farmers.

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: A country in sub-Saharan Africa where the study was conducted.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region where poverty and low agricultural productivity are associated with gender yield gaps.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A state of being extremely poor, associated with low agricultural productivity in smallholder farmers.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input, affected by gender yield gaps.

entities.name: EXOGENOUS SWITCHING REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical model used to analyze the gender yield gap.

entities.name: OAXACA-BLINDER DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: A framework used to decompose the gender yield gap into different sources.

entities.name: MALE HEADED HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: Households led by men, which showed a yield advantage in the study.

entities.name: FEMALE HEADED HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: Households led by women, who face a yield gap compared to male-headed households.

entities.name: RESOURCES AND ENDOWMENTS
entities.description: The assets and inputs available to farmers, identified as the main source of the gender yield gap.

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY VALIDATION TRIALS
entities.description: Activities including testing and demonstrations used to augment yields and close the gender gap.
